case_title: CALIFORNIA COASTAL PROTECTION NETWORK, A VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, A MUNICIPAL et al

docket_entries.description: NOTICE OF TAKING DEMURRER OFF CALENDAR FILED BY PLAINTIFF CALIFORNIA COASTAL PROTECTION NETWORK, A CALIFORNIA NON-PROFIT PUBLIC BENEFIT CORPORATION

LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF CALIFORNIA COASTAL PROTECTION NETWORK'S DEMURRER TO ANSWER TO COMPLAINT IS OFF CALENDAR. AN AMENDED ANSWER WAS FILED ON OCTOBER 12, 2011. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN, REPORTER: LAVENA WARD, CSR #7077

LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF CALIFORNIA COASTAL PROTECTION NETWORK'S DEMURRER TO THE FIRST AMENDED ANSWER TO COMPLAINT OF CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: THE DEMURRER SERVES NO USEFUL PURPOSE. PLAINTIFF SHOULD PROPOUND DISCOVERY TO DETERMINE WHICH, IF ANY, OF THE AFFIRMATIVE DEFENSES DEFENDANT HAS ANY BASIS TO PURSUE. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE HAROLD KAHN; REPORTER: SONIA WONG, CSR #11288

LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF CALIFORNIA COASTAL PROTECTION NETWORK'S MOTION TO STRIKE PORTIONS OF DEFENDANTS FIRST AMENDED ANSWER. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: GRANTED AS TO THE 23RD DEFENSE AND DENIED AS TO THE THIRD, SIXTH AND SEVENTH DEFENSES. THE 23RD DEFENSE IS NOT A COGNIZABLE DEFENSE. THE BALANCE OF THE MOTION SERVES NO USEFUL PURPOSE. PLAINTIFF SHOULD PROPOUND DISCOVERY TO DETERMINE WHICH, IF ANY, OF THE AFFIRMATIVE DEFENSES DEFENDANT HAS ANY BASIS TO PURSUE. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE HAROLD KAHN; REPORTER: SONIA WONG, CSR #11288

MOTION TO STRIKE AMENDED ANSWER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Nov-23-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFF CALIFORNIA COASTAL PROTECTION NETWORK'S MOTION TO STRIKE PORTIONS OF DEFENDANTS FIRST AMENDED ANSWER.
Appearances: Thomas S. Lakritz of the Office of the City Attorney (554.6547) for defendant and responding party; Mark A. Massara of Conservation Strategies (665.7008) and Victor J. Otten of Otten & Joyce LLP (310.378.8533) for plaintiff and moving party.
Argued and the court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: granted as to the 23rd defense and denied as to the third, sixth and seventh defenses. The 23rd defense is not a cognizable defense. The balance of the motion serves no useful purpose. Plaintiff should propound discovery to determine which, if any, of the affirmative defenses defendant has any basis to pursue. Order signed. (302/HEK) Judge Harold Kahn; Reporter: Sonia Wong, CSR #11288

DEMURRER TO AMENDED ANSWER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Nov-23-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFF CALIFORNIA COASTAL PROTECTION NETWORK'S DEMURRER TO THE FIRST AMENDED ANSWER TO COMPLAINT OF CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO.
Appearances: Thomas S. Lakritz of the Office of the City Attorney (554.6547) for defendant and responding party; Mark A. Massara of Conservation Strategies (665.7008) and Victor J. Otten of Otten & Joyce LLP (310.378.8533) for plaintiff and moving party.
Argued and the court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: the demurrer serves no useful purpose. Plaintiff should propound discovery to determine which, if any, of the affirmative defenses defendant has any basis to pursue. Order signed. (302/HEK) Judge Harold Kahn; Reporter: Sonia Wong, CSR #11288

DEMURRER TO ANSWER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Oct-25-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling -
PLAINTIFF CALIFORNIA COASTAL PROTECTION NETWORK'S DEMURRER TO ANSWER TO COMPLAINT is Off calendar. An amended answer was filed on October 12, 2011. Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR #7077 = (302/HEK)
